audit_items.proof_trace: All dereferences of the primary `str` parameter are guarded by explicit `if (str)` checks in every function (str2upper L115, str2upperbv L132, str2lower L150, str2lowerbv L167). The `bv` parameter in str2upperbv/str2lowerbv is checked only via `assert(bv != NULL)` (disabled in release builds), but no internal callers exist in the codebase and no attacker-controlled LDAP path can supply a NULL `bv` pointer — callers always pass the address of a stack-allocated struct. The `(s - str)` arithmetic when both are NULL is undefined behavior but not a pointer dereference.
